Profile

Dylan Lawless, PhD · Zürich

Dylan Lawless

Senior scientist in statistical genomics and translational data science. I build statistical methods, omics workflows, scientific software, and regulated data infrastructure for biomedical research, healthcare, and biotech.

My work connects genomics, multi-omics, clinical data, and real-world evidence through reproducible systems that make complex biomedical data usable, auditable, and reusable.

Download CV as PDF Contact

Profile

I work at the intersection of statistical genomics, translational data science, scientific software, and regulated biomedical infrastructure. I have led analytical delivery across hospital, academic, and product-facing environments, including clinical genomics workflows, multi-omics studies, secure data systems, Bayesian methods, variant interpretation frameworks, and user-facing biomedical software.

My focus is evidence that can be defended: reproducible analyses, explicit uncertainty, traceable data systems, and software that supports decisions beyond the original research setting.

Selected impact

1,000 patients in regulated clinical workflows
5,000 participants in translational studies
100,000+ whole genomes in biobank-scale analyses
100 TB+ secure biomedical data analysed

Role fit

Statistical genomics

Bayesian inference, GWAS, rare variant analysis, uncertainty quantification, population-scale genomics, and reproducible statistical workflows.

Translational data science

Integration of genomics, multi-omics, clinical data, real-world evidence, and biomedical decision systems.

Biomedical software

Scientific packages, structured reporting, full-stack platforms, authenticated systems, and machine-readable outputs.

Regulated infrastructure

Secure computing, traceability, audit-ready workflows, data governance, and hospital-facing analytical systems.

Selected experience

Universitäts-Kinderspital Zürich, senior scientist, 2023 to present

Genomics lead within the Swiss Pediatric Sepsis Study and SwissPedHealth National Data Stream. Built and delivered secure workflows linking whole-genome sequencing, multi-omics, electronic health record data, and high-performance computing across hospital settings.

Focus areas include rare disease diagnostics, paediatric sepsis, clinical genomics infrastructure, secure biomedical data delivery, and translational evidence systems.

EPFL Global Health Institute, senior postdoctoral scientist, 2018 to 2023

Developed statistical genomics and multi-omics workflows across sepsis, tuberculosis, chronic inflammatory disease, and translational cohort studies. Worked across EPFL, ETH Zurich, Swiss TPH, and international partner institutions.

Focus areas included genomic association studies, rare variant analyses, high-dimensional modelling, and integrative biomedical data analysis.

University of Leeds, PhD candidate, 2015 to 2018

Led genomic discovery and functional validation in rare immune disease in a translational hospital setting. Investigated severe clinical cases using genome sequencing, molecular biology, and clinical integration.

Work contributed to novel disease discovery and treatment-related findings in immune dysregulation, autoinflammation, and immunodeficiency.

ACM Global Laboratories, analytical scientist, 2014 to 2015

Worked in a regulated bioanalytical laboratory supporting global clinical trial programmes, biomarker workflows, and quality-controlled assay delivery.

Selected technologies

  • PanelAppRex AI: harmonised disease-gene panels from structured clinical and genetic queries, preprinted in Quant Group et al. (2025).
  • VCFheader: parsing VCF headers and generating structured standalone HTML reports, described in Lawless (2026).
View all technologies

Technical range

Statistics and inference

Bayesian inference, probabilistic modelling, causal inference, GWAS, rare variant association testing, uncertainty quantification, and multi-omics integration.

Scientific software

R, Python, C, Rust, package development, reproducible workflows, machine-readable reporting, Git, and command-line tools.

Biomedical platforms

Next.js, React, TypeScript, SQL, PostgreSQL, Supabase, Vercel, authenticated systems, controlled file transfer, and reporting workflows.

Secure infrastructure

High-performance computing, AWS, Azure, Unix systems, Docker, Singularity, Nextflow, Snakemake, encrypted storage, and regulated data environments.

Selected publications

ORCID record: 0000-0001-8496-3725

Application of qualifying variants for genomic analysis. Dylan Lawless, Ali Saadat, Mariam Ait Oumelloul, Simon Boutry, Veronika Stadler, Sabine Österle, Jan Armida, David Haerry, D. Sean Froese, Luregn J. Schlapbach, Jacques Fellay. Bioinformatics, 42(2), btaf676, 2026. DOI: 10.1093/bioinformatics/btaf676

Archipelago method for variant set association test statistics. Dylan Lawless, Ali Saadat, Mariam Ait Oumelloul, Luregn J. Schlapbach, Jacques Fellay. Genetic Epidemiology, 50(1), e70025, 2026. DOI: 10.1002/gepi.70025

Quantifying prior probabilities for disease-causing variants reveals the top genetic contributors in inborn errors of immunity. Quant Group, Simon Boutry, Ali Saadat, Maarja Soomann, Johannes Trück, D. Sean Froese, Jacques Fellay, Sinisa Savic, Luregn J. Schlapbach, Dylan Lawless. medRxiv,, 2025. DOI: 10.1101/2025.03.25.25324607

PanelAppRex aggregates disease gene panels and facilitates sophisticated search. Quant Group, Simon Boutry, Ali Saadat, Sinisa Savic, Luregn J. Schlapbach, Jacques Fellay, Dylan Lawless. medRxiv,, 2025. DOI: 10.1101/2025.03.20.25324319

Rare variants in infection response protein pathways associated with sepsis in children. Dylan Lawless, Pauline Rogg, Manon Bouzereau, Robin Fallegger, Zaira Seferbekova, Valeriia Timonina, Konstantin Popadin, Ali Saadat, Zhi Ming Xu, Simon Boutry, Christian W. Thorball, Flavia Hodel, Alessandro Borghesi, Johannes Trück, Martin Stocker, Klara M. Posfay-Barbe, Ulrich Heininger, Sara Bernhard-Stirnemann, Anita Niederer-Loher, Christian Kahlert, Giancarlo Natalucci, Christa Relly, Thomas Riedel, Christoph Aebi, Christoph Berger, Eric Giannoni, Philipp Agyeman, Jacques Fellay, Luregn J. Schlapbach. medRxiv,, 2025. DOI: 10.1101/2025.06.12.25329504

A case of adult-onset Still’s disease caused by a novel splicing mutation in TNFAIP3 successfully treated with tocilizumab. Dylan Lawless, Shelly Pathak, Thomas Edward Scambler, Lylia Ouboussad, Rashida Anwar, Sinisa Savic. Frontiers in Immunology, 9, 2018. DOI: 10.3389/fimmu.2018.01527

Prevalence and clinical challenges among adult primary immunodeficiency patients with RAG deficiency. Dylan Lawless, Christopher B. Geier, Jennifer R. Farmer, Hana Lango Allen, Daniel Thwaites, Fatma Atschekzei, Michael Brown, David Buchbinder, Siobhan O. Burns, Manish J. Butte, Krisztian Csomos, Sai V. V. Deevi, William Egner, Stephan Ehl, Sinisa Savic. Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology,, 2018. DOI: 10.1016/j.jaci.2018.02.007

Predicting the occurrence of variants in RAG1 and RAG2. Dylan Lawless, Hana Lango Allen, James Thaventhiran, Flavia Hodel, Rashida Anwar, Jacques Fellay, Jolan E. Walter, Sinisa Savic. Journal of Clinical Immunology, 39(7), 688–701, 2019. DOI: 10.1007/s10875-019-00670-z

Germline TET2 loss of function causes childhood immunodeficiency and lymphoma. Jarmila Stremenova Spegarova, Dylan Lawless, Siti Mardhiana Binti Mohamad, Karin R. Engelhardt, Gina Doody, Jennifer Shrimpton, Anne Rensing-Ehl, Stephan Ehl, Frederic Rieux-Laucat, Catherine Cargo, Helen Griffin, Aneta Mikulasova, Meghan Acres, Neil V. Morgan, James A. Poulter, Eamonn G. Sheridan, Philip Chetcuti, Sean O’Riordan, Rashida Anwar, Clive R. Carter, Stefan Przyborski, Kevin Windebank, Andrew J. Cant, Majlinda Lako, Chris M. Bacon, Sinisa Savic, Sophie Hambleton. Blood, 136(9), 1055–1066, 2020. DOI: 10.1182/blood.2020005844

Familial autoinflammation with neutrophilic dermatosis reveals a regulatory mechanism of pyrin activation. Seth L. Masters, V. Lagou, Isabelle Jéru, P. J. Baker, L. Van Eyck, D. A. Parry, Dylan Lawless, D. De Nardo, J. E. Garcia-Perez, L. F. Dagley, C. L. Holley, J. Dooley, F. Moghaddas, E. Pasciuto, P. Y. Jeandel, R. Sciot, D. Lyras, A. I. Webb, S. E. Nicholson, L. De Somer, E. Van Nieuwenhove, J. Ruuth-Praz, B. Copin, E. Cochet, M. Medlej-Hashim, A. Megarbane, K. Schroder, Sinisa Savic, A. Goris, S. Amselem, C. Wouters, A. Liston. Science Translational Medicine, 8(332), 2016. DOI: 10.1126/scitranslmed.aaf1471

Education and recognition

Education

PhD in Medicine, University of Leeds.
MSc in Immunology, Trinity College Dublin, first class honours.
BSc in Microbiology, University College Cork.

Selected recognition

FKZ Children’s Research Centre research prize.
Microsoft Azure Research Award, Data Science and ML in Predictive Genomics.
Wellcome Genome Campus visitor research grant.
University of Leeds 110th Anniversary Postgraduate Research Scholarship.
